title: rubus badius
intro: Rubus badius, also known as the Western Woodland Bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Rubus badius lie in its leaves and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus badius include reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as aiding in digestion and treating diarrhea. Herbal preparations of Rubus badius can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
